 {"id":"ft-4iowo","title":"[audit][T9.6] Fix SSH config path override handling","description":"# Fix SSH config path override handling

## Problem
frankenterm/ssh/src/sessioninner.rs line 194:
  sess.options_parse_config(None)?; // FIXME: overridden config path?

The SSH session always parses the default SSH config (~/.ssh/config) and ignores any user-specified config path override. This means operators who use non-standard SSH config locations cannot configure SSH behavior for ft.

## Fix
Thread the config path override from ft.toml [ssh] section through to the libssh session initialization.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Custom SSH config path is respected
2. FIXME comment removed
3. Default behavior (None = ~/.ssh/config) preserved when no override specified","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:24.181283Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T20:01:04.189390Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T20:01:04.188848Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ssh","vendored"]}
 {"id":"ft-4ja5","title":"Progressive disclosure: default/verbose/debug output levels","description":"# Progressive disclosure (verbosity)

## Goal
Show essential information by default, with increasing detail available on request, without sacrificing scriptability.

## Key design rule
- `-v/-vv` controls **how much** information we include.
- `--format {auto|plain|json}` controls **how** we render it.

This keeps CLI behavior consistent and avoids a proliferation of ad-hoc `--json` flags.

## Verbosity levels
- Default (no flag): Essential info only
- `-v`: Additional context
- `-vv`: Debug details

## JSON mode
- Use `--format json` for machine-readable output.
- JSON output must be schema-stable and compatible with non-TTY usage.

## Example: `wa status`
```bash
# Default
$ wa status
Panes: 4 observed, 1 ignored
Events: 2 unhandled

# Verbose
$ wa status -v
Panes:
  1: codex [PromptActive] /home/user/project (last: 2m ago)
  3: claude [CommandRunning] /home/user/other (last: 5s ago)
  5: local [Ignored] (exclude rule: title=htop)
  7: gemini [PromptActive] /home/user/gemini (last: 30s ago)

Events (unhandled):
  - codex.usage_limit_reached (Pane 1, 2m ago)
  - session.compaction (Pane 3, 5m ago)

# Debug
$ wa status -vv
[as above, plus:]
Health:
  Queue depth: 0
  Ingest lag: 12ms
  DB size: 1.2GB
  Circuit breakers: all closed

# Machine
$ wa status --format json | jq .
```

## Implementation sketch
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
struct Cli {
    #[arg(short, long, action = ArgAction::Count)]
    verbose: u8,
}

fn format_status(status: &Status, verbosity: u8) -> String {
    match verbosity {
        0 => format_status_brief(status),
        1 => format_status_verbose(status),
        _ => format_status_debug(status),
    }
}
```

## Consistency rules
- All human commands support `-v` and `-vv`.
- Default shows what the user needs to know.
- Verbose adds context for understanding.
- Debug adds internal state for troubleshooting.
- `--format json` output must not contain ANSI escapes and must be schema-stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands support `-v/-vv`.
- [ ] Default output is clean and minimal.
- [ ] Verbose adds useful context.
- [ ] Debug shows internal state.
- [ ] Consistent pattern across all commands.

## Testing
- Unit tests for verbosity routing (brief/verbose/debug paths).
- Contract tests:
  - `--format json` schema validation
  - no ANSI escapes in non-TTY/plain/json
- Help/output snapshot tests where appropriate (normalized).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:23.666085891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.219265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T04:53:54.213378616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4jxq","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.

-{"id":"ft-4ohrd","title":"[audit][T8.3] Make CLICK_INTERVAL user-configurable for accessibility","description":"# Make CLICK_INTERVAL user-configurable for accessibility

## Problem
frankenterm/term/src/input.rs line 68:
  CLICK_INTERVAL = Duration::from_millis(500)

This controls the maximum time between clicks for double-click detection. 500ms is the industry standard default but is NOT suitable for all users:
- Users with motor impairments may need 1000-2000ms
- Power users with fast reflexes may prefer 300ms
- Accessibility guidelines (WCAG, ADA) recommend this be configurable

## Fix
1. Expose click_interval_ms in the vendored config system
2. Default to 500ms
3. Document in accessibility section

## Why priority 2 (not 3 like other vendored)
Accessibility is a higher-priority concern than other vendored constants. This is a user-facing interaction parameter that directly affects usability for people with disabilities.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. CLICK_INTERVAL reads from config
2. Default is 500ms
3. Documented as accessibility-relevant setting","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeMill","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:32.733301Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T20:06:53.994207Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","audit","config","vendored"],"comments":[{"id":2508,"issue_id":"ft-4ohrd","author":"BronzeMill","text":"Claimed by BronzeMill.  {"id":"ft-4psw","title":"FTUI-06.4 Migrate filter and search input widgets with robust editing semantics","description":"## Background\nText input regressions in filters/search dramatically degrade operator workflow quality.\n\n## Deliverables\n- reusable text input primitives for filters/search\n- cursor/editing behavior parity (backspace, movement, clear, focus transitions)\n- view integration for panes/events/search surfaces\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- editing semantics are deterministic and tested\n- filter/search interactions remain fast and predictable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:08:30.688930506Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.261593Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T03:32:03.902571830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4psw","depends_on_id":"ft-i659","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-4r7","title":"Multi-account exhaustion handling: all accounts at limit → graceful degradation","description":"# Multi-account exhaustion handling

## Purpose
Handle the scenario where ALL configured accounts have reached their usage limits simultaneously.

## Problem
Current usage limit workflow assumes at least one account is available for failover.
When all accounts are exhausted, we need graceful degradation:
1. Don't attempt impossible failovers
2. Provide clear status to user
3. Schedule auto-resume at earliest reset time
4. Support notification of this critical state

## Detection
```rust
// In account selection logic
let available = accounts.iter()
    .filter(|a| a.percent_remaining > 0.0 || a.reset_time < now)
    .collect::<Vec<_>>();

if available.is_empty() {
    return Err(AccountExhausted {
        next_reset: accounts.iter()
            .map(|a| a.reset_time)
            .min(),
        accounts_checked: accounts.len(),
    });
}
```

## Workflow Behavior
When `AccountExhausted` detected:

### Step 1: Capture state
- Save current session transcript
- Record in-progress task (if determinable)
- Log which accounts were checked

### Step 2: Calculate resume time
```rust
let earliest_reset = accounts.iter()
    .filter_map(|a| a.reset_time)
    .min();
```

### Step 3: Persist "awaiting reset" state
```sql
INSERT INTO workflow_paused 
(pane_id, reason, resume_at, state_snapshot)
VALUES (?, 'all_accounts_exhausted', ?, ?);
```

### Step 4: Notify user
```
🔴 All accounts exhausted

All 3 configured OpenAI accounts have reached their usage limits.

Next reset: account-2 @ 2026-01-19 00:00 UTC (6h 23m)

Options:
  1. Wait for reset (wa will auto-resume if enabled)
  2. Add new account: wa accounts add
  3. Continue manually in exhausted account (with limits)

Status: wa accounts status
Resume: wa workflow resume --when-available
```

### Step 5: Schedule resume (optional)
If auto-resume enabled:
- Create timer/cron for earliest reset time
- Resume workflow when triggered

## Configuration
```toml
[accounts.exhaustion]
notify_channels = ["desktop", "webhook"]
auto_resume_on_reset = true
save_transcript = true
```

## Robot Mode Error
```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "E_ALL_ACCOUNTS_EXHAUSTED",
    "message": "All configured accounts have reached usage limits",
    "data": {
      "accounts_checked": 3,
      "next_reset": "2026-01-19T00:00:00Z",
      "next_reset_account": "account-2"
    }
  }
}
```

## Testing
- Unit: Account selection returns exhaustion error correctly
- Unit: Resume time calculation
- Integration: Workflow pauses correctly
- E2E: Full exhaustion → notification → resume flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Exhaustion detected when all accounts at limit
- [ ] Clear user notification with reset times
- [ ] State preserved for resume
- [ ] Auto-resume works when enabled
- [ ] Robot mode returns structured error","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:14.002558283Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:30:34.037935162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:30:34.037786926Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4r7","depends_on_id":"ft-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-4r7","depends_on_id":"ft-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-4r7","depends_on_id":"ft-nu4.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-4r7","depends_on_id":"ft-nu4.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
